briefdescription.para: Window size type @SINCE_1_2.60.
Window position type @SINCE_2_1.45.
Window focus signal type @SINCE_1_4.35.
Window resized signal type @SINCE_1_4.35.
Key event signal type @SINCE_1_9.21.
Touch signal type @SINCE_1_9.28.
Creates an initialized handle to a new Window. @SINCE_1_0.0.

parameterdescription.para: The position and size of the Window

simplesect.para: A new window
This creates an extra window in addition to the default main window

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized handle to a new Window. @SINCE_2_2.33.

simplesect.para: This creates an extra window in addition to the default main window
A new Window

briefdescription.para: Downcast sceneHolder to window.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_1.46
handle
The handle need to downcast
Whether it's a valid window or not

briefdescription.para: Creates an uninitialized handle.

detaileddescription.para: This can be initialized using Dali::Application::GetWindow() or Dali::Window::New(). @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
A reference to the copied handle
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.
Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.24
rhs
A reference to the moved handle
A reference to this handle

briefdescription.para: Adds a child Actor to the Window.

detaileddescription.para: The child will be referenced.

@SINCE_1_4.19
actor
The child
The actor has been initialized.
The actor does not have a parent.

briefdescription.para: Removes a child Actor from the Window.

detaileddescription.para: The child will be unreferenced.

@SINCE_1_4.19
actor
The child
The actor has been added to the stage.

briefdescription.para: Sets the background color of the Window.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_4.19
color
The new background color
If this window is transparent, the color is converted premultiplied alpha format internally.

briefdescription.para: Gets the background color of the Window.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_4.19
The background color

briefdescription.para: Returns the root Layer of the Window.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_4.19
The root layer

briefdescription.para: Returns the overlay Layer of the Window. If there isn't overlay layer yet, this method create overlay layer and exclusive render task internally.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_2.10
The root layer

briefdescription.para: Queries the number of on-scene layers in the Window.

detaileddescription.para: Note that a default layer is always provided (count >= 1).

@SINCE_1_4.19
The number of layers

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the layer at a specified depth in the Window.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_4.19
depth
The depth
The layer found at the given depth
Depth is less than layer count; see GetLayerCount().

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the DPI of the window.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.21
The DPI of the window

briefdescription.para: Sets the window name and class string. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Raises window to the top of Window stack. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Lowers window to the bottom of Window stack. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Activates window to the top of Window stack even it is iconified. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Adds an orientation to the list of available orientations. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Removes an orientation from the list of available orientations. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Sets a preferred orientation. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: Orientation is in the list of available orientations.
To unset the preferred orientation, orientation should be set NO_ORIENTATION_PREFERENCE.

briefdescription.para: Gets the preferred orientation. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The preferred orientation if previously set, or none

briefdescription.para: Gets the native handle of the window.

detaileddescription.para:
When users call this function, it wraps the actual type used by the underlying window system. @SINCE_1_0.0
The native handle of the Window or an empty handle

briefdescription.para: Sets whether window accepts focus or not.
Returns whether window accepts focus or not.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.60
True if the window accept focus, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Shows the window if it is hidden. @SINCE_1_2.60.
Hides the window if it is showing. @SINCE_1_2.60.
Returns whether the window is visible or not. @SINCE_1_2.60.

simplesect.para: True if the window is visible, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets the count of supported auxiliary hints of the window. @SINCE_1_2.60.

simplesect.para: The number of supported auxiliary hints.

The window auxiliary hint is the value which is used to decide which actions should be made available to the user by the window manager. If you want to set specific hint to your window, then you should check whether it exists in the supported auxiliary hints.

briefdescription.para: Gets the supported auxiliary hint string of the window. @SINCE_1_2.60.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the supported auxiliary hint lists

simplesect.para: The auxiliary hint string of the index.

The window auxiliary hint is the value which is used to decide which actions should be made available to the user by the window manager. If you want to set specific hint to your window, then you should check whether it exists in the supported auxiliary hints.

briefdescription.para: Creates an auxiliary hint of the window. @SINCE_1_2.60.

simplesect.para: The ID of created auxiliary hint, or 0 on failure.

briefdescription.para: Removes an auxiliary hint of the window. @SINCE_1_2.60.

parameterdescription.para: The ID of the auxiliary hint.

simplesect.para: True if no error occurred, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Changes a value of the auxiliary hint. @SINCE_1_2.60.

parameterdescription.para: The value string to be set.

simplesect.para: True if no error occurred, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets a value of the auxiliary hint. @SINCE_1_2.60.

simplesect.para: The string value of the auxiliary hint ID, or an empty string if none exists.

briefdescription.para: Gets a ID of the auxiliary hint string. @SINCE_1_2.60.

simplesect.para: The ID of the auxiliary hint string, or 0 if none exists.

briefdescription.para: Sets a region to accept input events. @SINCE_1_2.60.

parameterdescription.para: The region to accept input events.

briefdescription.para: Sets a window type. @SINCE_2_0.0.

simplesect.para: The default window type is NORMAL.

briefdescription.para: Gets a window type. @SINCE_2_0.0.

simplesect.para: A window type.

briefdescription.para: Sets a priority level for the specified notification window. @SINCE_2_0.0.

simplesect.para: The result of the window operation. @PRIVLEVEL_PUBLIC @PRIVILEGE_WINDOW_PRIORITY
This can be used for a notification type window only. The default level is NotificationLevel::NONE.

briefdescription.para: Gets a priority level for the specified notification window. @SINCE_2_0.0.

simplesect.para: The notification window level.
This can be used for a notification type window only.

briefdescription.para: Sets a transparent window's visual state to opaque.

detaileddescription.para:
If a visual state of a transparent window is opaque, then the window manager could handle it as an opaque window when calculating visibility. @SINCE_1_2.60
opaque
Whether the window's visual state is opaque.
This will have no effect on an opaque window. It doesn't change transparent window to opaque window but lets the window manager know the visual state of the window.

briefdescription.para: Returns whether a transparent window's visual state is opaque or not. @SINCE_1_2.60.

simplesect.para: True if the window's visual state is opaque, false otherwise.
The return value has no meaning on an opaque window.

briefdescription.para: Sets a window's screen off mode.

detaileddescription.para:
This API is useful when the application needs to keep the display turned on. If the application sets the screen mode to #Dali::WindowScreenOffMode::NEVER to its window and the window is shown, the window manager requests the display system to keep the display on as long as the window is shown. If the window is no longer shown, then the window manager requests the display system to go back to normal operation. @SINCE_2_0.0
screenOffMode
The screen mode.
The result of the window operation. @PRIVLEVEL_PUBLIC @PRIVILEGE_DISPLAY

briefdescription.para: Gets a screen off mode of the window. @SINCE_2_0.0.

simplesect.para: The screen off mode.

briefdescription.para: Sets preferred brightness of the window.

detaileddescription.para:
This API is useful when the application needs to change the brightness of the screen when it is appeared on the screen. If the brightness has been set and the window is shown, the window manager requests the display system to change the brightness to the provided value. If the window is no longer shown, then the window manager requests the display system to go back to default brightness. A value less than 0 results in default brightness and a value greater than 100 results in maximum brightness. @SINCE_1_2.60
brightness
The preferred brightness (0 to 100).
The result of the window operation. @PRIVLEVEL_PUBLIC @PRIVILEGE_DISPLAY

briefdescription.para: Gets preferred brightness of the window. @SINCE_1_2.60.

simplesect.para: The preferred brightness.

briefdescription.para: Sets a size of the window.
Gets a size of the window.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.60
The size of the window

briefdescription.para: Sets a position of the window.
Gets a position of the window.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.60
The position of the window

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int numCols, unsigned int numRows, unsigned int column, unsigned int row, unsigned int colSpan, unsigned int rowSpan)
memberdef.name: SetLayout

briefdescription.para: Sets the layout of the window.

detaileddescription.para: This method sets the layout of the window based on the specified number of columns and rows, as well as the position and size of the window within that layout.

parameterdescription.para: The number of columns in the layout.
The number of rows in the layout.
The column number of the window within the layout.
The row number of the window within the layout.
The number of columns the window should span within the layout.
The number of rows the window should span within the layout. @SINCE_2_2.20

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the window is transparent or not.
Retrieves the list of render-tasks in the window.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.21
A valid handle to a RenderTaskList

briefdescription.para: Keeps rendering for at least the given amount of time.

detaileddescription.para: By default, Dali will stop rendering when no Actor positions are being set, and when no animations are running etc. This method is useful to force screen refreshes.

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the window has a depth buffer or not.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_5.20
enabled
True if a depth buffer should be created.
This is an alternative to setting the environment variable. There is no longer a global setting; the environment variable will only affect the main window (the automatically generated window).

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the window has a depth buffer.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_5.20
True if the window has a depth buffer

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the window has a depth buffer or not.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_5.20
enabled
True if a depth buffer should be created.
This is an alternative to setting the environment variable. There is no longer a global setting; the environment variable will only affect the main window (the automatically generated window).

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the window has a stencil buffer.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_5.20
True if the window has a stencil buffer

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the window has MSAA.
Queries whether the window has MSAA.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_5.20
True if the window has MSAA

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the window will update partial area or full area.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_2.33
enabled
True if the window should update partial area
This is an alternative to setting the environment variable. There is no longer a global setting; the environment variable will only affect the main window (the automatically generated window)

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the window will update partial area.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_2.33
True if the window should update partial area

briefdescription.para: The user should connect to this signal to get a timing when window gains focus or loses focus.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,boolfocusIn); The parameter is true if window gains focus, otherwise false. and window means this signal was called from what window

@SINCE_1_4.35
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the window is resized.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,intwidth,intheight); The parameters are the resized width and height. and window means this signal was called from what window

@SINCE_1_4.35
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when key event is received.

detaileddescription.para: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,KeyEventevent);

@SINCE_1_9.21
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the screen is touched and when the touch ends (i.e. the down & up touch events only).

detaileddescription.para: If there are multiple touch points, then this will be emitted when the first touch occurs and then when the last finger is lifted. An interrupted event will also be emitted (if it occurs). A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,TouchEventevent);

briefdescription.para: The window class is used internally for drawing.

detaileddescription.para: A Window has an orientation and indicator properties. You can get a valid Window handle by calling Dali::Application::GetWindow(). @SINCE_1_0.0
